title: Plant growth-promoting microorganisms as a biotechnological strategy for the biofortification of agricultural products.
author_string: Chávez-Díaz, Ismael F, Zelaya-Molina, Lily X, Ortega-García, Marisel, Rios-Rocaful, Yoania, Blanco-Camarillo, Mario et al.
journal_full: Plant science : an international journal of experimental plant biology
abstract: Given the escalating global demand for nutritious and sustainably produced food, microbial biofortification using plant growth-promoting microorganisms (PGPMs) is emerging as a critical and eco-friendly agricultural strategy. This review synthesizes current knowledge on how PGPMs modify plant biochemistry, enhancing the uptake and assimilation of essential nutrients and stimulating the biosynthesis of bioactive compounds that significantly improve the nutritional and nutraceutical profile of crops. Beyond increasing nutrient density, PGPMs also modulate sensory quality attributes such as taste, aroma, and texture, and extend post-harvest shelf life by improving physiological stability and stress tolerance. Compared with conventional fortification methods dependent on synthetic inputs, microbial biofortification offers a more sustainable and environmentally compatible alternative, promoting soil health, nutrient cycling, and ecosystem resilience. By contributing to global food security and aligning with key Sustainable Development Goals, this approach bridges agricultural productivity with human nutrition. Finally, this article outlines the current challenges and identifies future research priorities, such as the integration of omics technologies and the development of robust microbial consortia, necessary to optimize and scale this sustainable innovation across diverse cropping systems.
